title: NLP practical tasks: NER, summarization, MT + DSA Graphs
focus: BIO tagging vs span-based; ROUGE / BERTScore / LLM-as-judge; beam search.

items.label: Walls And Gates
interviewQuestions: Is this BFS, DFS, or Union-Find? Defend the choice over the other two.
Walk through complexity in terms of V and E. Where do those costs come from?
How would you handle disconnected components, self-loops, or duplicate edges?

items.label: Course Schedule
interviewQuestions: Compare BFS-based topological sort (Kahn's) vs DFS with cycle detection.
How does this extend to course-schedule-ii where you must output the order?

items.label: Named-entity recognition: BIO tagging, span-based
interviewQuestions: Compare BIO tagging vs span-based NER models.
How would you evaluate a NER model — token-level F1 vs entity-level F1, and which actually matters?

items.label: Extractive vs abstractive summarization
interviewQuestions: How would you evaluate a summarization model — ROUGE vs BERTScore vs LLM-as-judge?
Compare extractive vs abstractive summarization — when is each the right tool?

items.label: Machine translation: BLEU, beam search
interviewQuestions: Why is beam search standard for translation but not for open-ended generation?
What is BLEU actually measuring, and where does it mislead?

items.label: Sentiment analysis: baselines, transformers, and failure modes
interviewQuestions: Design a sentiment classifier starting from TF-IDF + logistic regression and then a transformer baseline.
How would you handle negation, sarcasm, domain shift, and class imbalance in sentiment analysis?
Which metric would you report for an imbalanced sentiment dataset and why?
